title: corydalis solida subsp. subremota
intro: Corydalis solida subsp. subremota is a subspecies of flowering plant in the poppy family, Papaveraceae, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It is a perennial herb with a history of use in traditional medicine, valued for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant contains a range of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and glycosides, which have been shown to exhibit potential health benefits, including pain relief, reduced inflammation, and improved mood. Corydalis solida subsp. subremota is traditionally prepared as a tincture or infusion for its medicinal uses, although modern herbal preparations may also include standardized extracts or capsules.
